quiz_questions.question_text: A motor does 600 J of work in 3 seconds. What is the average power output?
A force of 50 N moves an object at a constant velocity of 4 m/s in the direction of the force. What is the instantaneous power?
An engine rated at 500 W runs for 10 seconds. How much work does it do?
A machine takes in 1000 J of energy and produces 600 J of useful work. What is its efficiency?
quiz_questions.explanation: Efficiency = useful work output / total energy input = 600/1000 = 0.60 = 60%.

quiz_questions.question_text: A pump lifts 20 kg of water to a height of 5 m in 4 seconds. What is its power output? (Use g = 10 m/s²)
How many watts are in 1 horsepower?
quiz_questions.explanation: 1 horsepower = 746 watts.

quiz_questions.question_text: A car engine produces 150 kW while the car travels at 30 m/s. What is the total driving force?
A 60 kg person climbs 5 m of stairs in 10 s. What is their average power output? (Use g = 10 m/s²)
A 2 kW motor is 80% efficient. What is its useful power output?
quiz_questions.explanation: Useful output = efficiency × input = 0.80 × 2 = 1.6 kW.

quiz_questions.question_text: A force F = 10t N (where t is in seconds) acts on an object moving at constant velocity 3 m/s. What is the instantaneous power at t = 4 s?
What is power in physics?

options.text: The rate at which work is done or energy is transferred: P = dW/dt
The total amount of work done on a system
The force applied to an object over a distance
The product of mass and acceleration

quiz_questions.explanation: Power is the rate of doing work or transferring energy. P = dW/dt. The SI unit is the watt (W).

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a watt (W)?

options.text: The SI unit of power, equal to 1 joule per second
The unit of energy equal to 1 joule per meter
The unit of efficiency in percentage form
The unit of force equal to 1 newton per second

quiz_questions.explanation: The watt is the SI unit of power. 1 W = 1 J/s.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is average power?

options.text: The total work done divided by the total time interval: P_avg = W/Δt
The power output when force and velocity are perpendicular
The power at a specific instant in time
The maximum power an engine can deliver

quiz_questions.explanation: Average power is the total work done divided by the total time taken: P_avg = W/Δt.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is instantaneous power?

options.text: The total work done divided by total time
The power output when an engine first starts
The power at a specific moment, given by P = F · v or P = dW/dt
The power lost to friction in a system

quiz_questions.explanation: Instantaneous power is the rate of doing work at a specific moment: P = dW/dt = F · v.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is efficiency?

options.text: The difference between input power and output power
The rate at which a system produces power
The ratio of useful energy output to total energy input, often expressed as a percentage
The total energy output of a system

quiz_questions.explanation: Efficiency = useful energy (or power) output / total energy (or power) input. It is always between 0 and 1 (or 0% and 100%).

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a horsepower (hp)?

options.text: A unit of force equal to 746 newtons
A unit of power equal to 746 watts, commonly used for engines and motors
A unit of torque used in automotive engineering
A unit of energy equal to 746 joules

quiz_questions.explanation: One horsepower (hp) equals 746 watts. It is a traditional unit of power used especially for engines and motors.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does P = F · v represent?

options.text: The instantaneous power delivered by a force F to an object moving at velocity v
The work done by a force over a distance
The momentum change of an object
The kinetic energy of an object

quiz_questions.explanation: P = F · v gives the instantaneous power, which is the dot product of force and velocity at a given moment.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the kilowatt-hour (kWh)?

options.text: A unit of energy equal to the energy delivered by 1 kW of power over 1 hour (3.6 × 10⁶ J)
A measure of how fast electricity is consumed
A unit of power commonly used in electricity

quiz_questions.explanation: A kilowatt-hour is a unit of energy: 1 kWh = 1000 W × 3600 s = 3.6 × 10⁶ J. It measures total energy, not power.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does it mean when we say power is the derivative of work with respect to time?

options.text: Power is always constant over time
Power and work have the same units
Work is the integral of force over distance
Power measures how quickly work accumulates at each instant: P = dW/dt

quiz_questions.explanation: P = dW/dt means that power at any instant is the rate at which work is being done at that moment.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is energy loss in a system?

options.text: The total energy in a system divided by time
The difference between energy input and useful energy output, typically converted to heat or sound
Energy that is destroyed and disappears

quiz_questions.explanation: Energy loss is the energy input minus useful output. This energy is not destroyed but converted to less useful forms like heat and sound.

quiz_questions.question_text: A 1200 kg car needs to climb a hill that rises 100 m over a 2 km road distance at a constant speed of 25 m/s. Road friction exerts a constant 400 N force. What engine power is required? (Use g = 10 m/s²)
quiz_questions.explanation: Slope angle: sin θ = 100/2000 = 0.05. Forces along incline: gravity component = mg sin θ = 1200(10)(0.05) = 600 N, friction = 400 N. Total = 1000 N. P = Fv = 1000 × 25 = 25,000 W.

quiz_questions.question_text: A crane lifts a 500 kg steel beam at 2 m/s to the top of a building 30 m high. The motor is 75% efficient. What is the electrical power input required? (Use g = 10 m/s²)
quiz_questions.explanation: Useful power = Fv = mg × v = 500(10)(2) = 10,000 W. Input power = useful/efficiency = 10,000/0.75 = 13,333 W.

quiz_questions.question_text: An escalator carries 30 passengers (average mass 70 kg each) through a vertical rise of 6 m in 40 seconds. What is the minimum power required? (Use g = 10 m/s²)
A solar panel has an area of 2 m² and receives 1000 W/m² of sunlight. If the panel is 20% efficient, what electrical power does it produce?
quiz_questions.explanation: Solar input = 1000 × 2 = 2000 W. Electrical output = 0.20 × 2000 = 400 W.

quiz_questions.question_text: An electric car's motor produces 80 kW. The car travels at 40 m/s on level ground against a drag force of 1500 N and rolling friction of 500 N. What is the net power available for acceleration?
quiz_questions.explanation: Power used against drag and friction = (1500 + 500) × 40 = 2000 × 40 = 80,000 W = 80 kW. This equals the motor power, so no power remains for acceleration.

quiz_questions.question_text: A hydroelectric plant converts the PE of water falling 50 m. If 2000 kg of water flows per second and the turbine is 85% efficient, what is the electrical power output? (Use g = 10 m/s²)
quiz_questions.explanation: Gravitational power = mgh/t = 2000(10)(50) = 1,000,000 W. At 85% efficiency: 0.85 × 1,000,000 = 850,000 W = 850 kW.

quiz_questions.question_text: A cyclist pedals up a 5% grade (rise/run = 0.05) at a constant 8 m/s. The combined mass of cyclist and bike is 80 kg, and air resistance exerts 20 N. What power must the cyclist produce? (Use g = 10 m/s²)
quiz_questions.explanation: For small angles, sin θ ≈ tan θ = 0.05. Gravity component along slope = mg sin θ = 80(10)(0.05) = 40 N. Total resistance = 40 + 20 = 60 N. P = Fv = 60 × 8 = 480 W.

quiz_questions.question_text: A factory uses a conveyor belt driven by a 5 kW motor at 90% efficiency. The belt moves at 2 m/s. What maximum friction force can the belt exert on packages?
A runner (65 kg) sprints up a 30 m high hill in 50 seconds. If their metabolic efficiency is 25%, how much total metabolic energy did they expend? (Use g = 10 m/s²)
quiz_questions.explanation: Useful work = mgh = 65(10)(30) = 19,500 J. At 25% efficiency: total metabolic energy = 19,500/0.25 = 78,000 J.

quiz_questions.question_text: A wind turbine has blades sweeping an area of 100 m². Wind speed is 10 m/s and air density is 1.2 kg/m³. If the turbine captures 30% of the wind's kinetic energy, what is the power output?
quiz_questions.explanation: Mass flow rate = ρAv = 1.2(100)(10) = 1200 kg/s. Wind power = ½(dm/dt)v² = ½(1200)(100) = 60,000 W. At 30% capture: P = 0.3 × 60,000 = 18,000 W.
